acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A contiguous batch (seq 1,2) in a single EVENTS message applies both events and emits no WANT.
- [ ] Buffered deferred events are eventually forwarded once their prev is known (test covers).
- [ ] Add regression test in src/daemon/repl/session.rs and/or gap_buffer.rs.

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/session.rs, src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs, src/core/event.rs
description:
**Problem**
When an EVENTS message contains multiple contiguous events for the same (namespace, origin), only the first event is verified as contiguous. Subsequent events are verified with expected_prev=None and become Deferred, then buffered. The gap buffer never promotes deferred events after durable advances, so those events can remain stuck and trigger unnecessary WANTs even though we already received them.

**Evidence**
- src/daemon/repl/session.rs: handle_events() computes expected_prev via expected_prev_head() using the durable watermark snapshot (not updated per-frame).
- src/core/event.rs: verify_event_frame() returns VerifiedEventAny::Deferred when expected_prev_head is None for seq>1.
- src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs: ingest_one() buffers deferred events and advance_durable_batch() does not re-check buffered deferred events.

**Why this hurts**
A peer that legitimately batches seq N and N+1 in a single EVENTS frame will cause seq N+1 to be buffered and never applied unless it is resent later. This can stall replication and produce spurious WANTs.
design:
**Design**
Option A (preferred): Track per-(namespace, origin) last-seen head within the current EVENTS batch. Use that to compute expected_prev_head for later frames in the same batch so contiguous batches verify as Contiguous.

Option B: Extend GapBuffer to promote deferred events when durable advances (e.g., add a flush_ready() that re-validates deferred prev against the new durable head). Call it after advance_durable_batch().

Whichever approach, ensure events already in the batch are not re-requested via WANT.
title: Replication ingest can stall on contiguous batches

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Rich realistic fixture tests assert note payload preservation, not just note IDs.
- [ ] Tombstone/deleted-dep fixture tests assert note payload preservation.
- [ ] The migration suite would fail if note bodies/authors/timestamps were corrupted while note IDs stayed stable.
description:
**Problem**
The realistic fixture suite still only proves note identity, not note payload preservation. Today the helper compares note IDs only, so a migration could keep the right note IDs but corrupt note content, author, or timestamp fields and still pass the realistic fixture tests.

**Files**: crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/legacy_store_corpus/*
design:
**Design**
Promote the realistic note checks from identity-only to payload-aware assertions.

1. Extend the note assertion helpers to read note content, author, and timestamp fields for the fixture notes we already depend on.
2. Assert payload preservation for the rich-workflow and tombstone/deleted-dep fixtures, not just note IDs.
3. Keep the checks focused on the checked-in realistic fixtures so they remain easy to audit.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 4cd04383: realistic migration fixtures now assert note payload identity, not just note IDs. Rich-workflow and tombstone fixtures verify note content, author, and timestamps survive rewrite and merge paths. --repo /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs

title: Migration follow-up: realistic fixture note assertions must cover payloads, not just IDs

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Opening wal.sqlite rejects symlinked files/dirs.
- [ ] Opening wal/<namespace> rejects symlinked dirs.
- [ ] Replay/fsck path traversal rejects symlinked wal dirs.
- [ ] Tests cover rejection paths.

**Files:** src/daemon/wal/index.rs, src/daemon/wal/segment.rs, src/daemon/wal/replay.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
description:
**Problem**
Filesystem safety rules in REALTIME_PLAN require rejecting symlinks for wal.sqlite and wal/<namespace> directories. Current implementation only checks meta.json for symlinks; WAL/index paths can be symlinks, allowing writes outside store_dir.

**Evidence**
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs: SqliteWalIndex::open() creates/opens index/wal.sqlite with no symlink check.
- src/daemon/wal/segment.rs: SegmentWriter::open() create_dir_all(wal/<namespace>) and create_segment() without rejecting symlinks.
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs: list_namespaces/list_segments iterate wal/ without symlink rejection.
- REALTIME_PLAN §15.1 mandates symlink rejection for meta.json, index/wal.sqlite, and wal/<namespace>/.

**Why this hurts**
Symlinked WAL/index paths defeat store_dir confinement and can lead to writes outside the intended store, violating safety assumptions.
design:
**Design**
1) Add a small helper (e.g., ensure_no_symlink(path)) and use it before opening wal.sqlite and before using wal/<namespace> paths.
2) In SqliteWalIndex::open(), reject if wal.sqlite is a symlink (or its parent index dir is a symlink).
3) In SegmentWriter::open()/create_segment(), reject if wal/<namespace> path is a symlink (or resolve symlink metadata).
4) In replay_index (list_namespaces/list_segments), reject symlinked wal dirs with a clear error.
5) Map errors to PathSymlinkRejected in IPC.

Add tests with tempdir + symlink that confirm rejection.
title: Symlink rejection missing for WAL/index paths

description: AdminFixture in tests/integration/daemon/admin.rs uses a checkpoints_enabled bool to control env setup. Align with type_design.md by replacing it with a small enum (Enabled/Disabled) or similar typed mode.
title: Replace AdminFixture checkpoints_enabled bool with typed enum

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Rust has variants or a typed known-kind representation for every well-known Go dep kind above
- [ ] Readiness predicate returns true only for `blocks`, `conditional-blocks`, and `waits-for`; `parent-child` stays structural/DAG-only
- [ ] CLI parsing accepts `bd dep add ... --type <kind>` for all known kinds once the CLI alias exists
- [ ] `waits-for` typed edge payload is designed or explicitly split into a child bead before close
- [ ] Unknown dep-kind behavior is explicit and tested
- [ ] `cargo test -p beads-core` green
- [ ] Broader touched-surface verification is run before close
closed_reason: Implemented the full audited Go dependency kind vocabulary and centralized readiness semantics; matched live ready-work paths for blocks, conditional-blocks, and waits-for only; split typed waits-for payload/gate behavior to beads-rs-12u7.2.13; verified touched surfaces.
description:
**Problem**
`DepKind` in Rust covered only a tiny subset of current Go beads. The first Floor 1 draft listed four missing names, but Go beads now has a larger well-known vocabulary and bounded custom dependency strings.

Implementing only `conditional-blocks`, `waits-for`, `tracks`, and `relates-to` leaves known current edge kinds unrepresentable and causes later graph/identity/approval work to reopen the same enum repeatedly.

**Design**
Add the full Go well-known dependency vocabulary to Rust:

- workflow/control: `blocks`, `parent-child`, `conditional-blocks`, `waits-for`
- association: `related`, `discovered-from`
- graph links: `replies-to`, `relates-to`, `duplicates`, `supersedes`
- entity: `authored-by`, `assigned-to`, `approved-by`, `attests`
- tracking: `tracks`
- references: `until`, `caused-by`, `validates`
- delegation: `delegated-from`

Readiness semantics must match the audited live ready-work paths. `blocks`, `conditional-blocks`, and `waits-for` affect ready-work calculation. `parent-child` is structural and DAG-enforced, but it does not by itself block a child from `ready` while the parent is open.

Unknown dep-kind policy is explicit: reject unknown kinds for this bead. Do not leave call sites parsing raw strings independently.

`waits-for` needs a typed edge payload for gate behavior (`all-children`, `any-children`, optional `spawner_id`). Gas City-compatible JSON may still expose the legacy edge metadata string where required. The payload/gate semantics are split to `beads-rs-12u7.2.13` so this bead remains vocabulary + readiness-predicate work.

**References**
- `docs/go-parity/FLOOR1_REPLAN.md`
- `docs/go-parity/primitives/dep-kinds.md`
- `~/vendor/github.com/gastownhall/beads/internal/types/types.go`
- `~/vendor/github.com/gastownhall/beads/internal/storage/issueops/blocked.go`
- `~/vendor/github.com/gastownhall/gascity/internal/beads/memstore.go`
- `~/vendor/github.com/gastownhall/gascity/internal/beads/caching_store.go`

notes.author: darin@darinsmcstudio2.lan
notes.content: 2026-05-01 source audit correction: vendor Go beads DependencyType.AffectsReadyWork() says parent-child affects ready work, but Go's live computeBlockedIDs/GetReadyWork SQL and Gas City's MemStore/CachingStore Ready implementations filter ready blockers to blocks, waits-for, and conditional-blocks. Treat parent-child as structural/DAG-only unless those live paths change. Typed waits-for gate payload split to beads-rs-12u7.2.13.

title: Floor 1: full DepKind vocabulary and readiness semantics
